nodes.title: Record each call's arguments
nodes.why: Tests often need to verify exactly what was passed in each call.
nodes.alternative: Track only the latest call arguments.
nodes.tradeoff: Full history is more useful for debugging, but it uses more memory.

nodes.title: Record the receiver (`this`) too
nodes.why: When used as an object method, this tells you which object triggered the call.
nodes.tradeoff: You get better insight for method calls, with a small state cost.

nodes.title: Make implementation optional
nodes.why: The spy works in two modes: recorder-only, or recorder + passthrough implementation.
nodes.alternative: Require `impl` and throw if it is missing.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible API, at the cost of one runtime type check.

nodes.title: Use `apply` to preserve call context
nodes.why: This keeps behavior correct when the spy is called as a method with a meaningful `this`.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more verbose, but avoids subtle context bugs.

nodes.title: Expose small helper methods
nodes.why: Helpers keep test assertions short and easier to read.
nodes.alternative: Have tests inspect `calls` arrays directly every time.
nodes.tradeoff: A slightly wider API, but much cleaner test code.

nodes.title: Provide a reset method
nodes.why: Resetting avoids leaked state between test cases.
nodes.alternative: Create a brand new spy for each test.
nodes.tradeoff: Convenient reuse, but teams must remember to call `reset` when needed.
